jktekxZ la[;k 8 ds 14-300 fd-eh- ls 42-000 fd-eh- rd ds [k.M ds laca/k esa izkf/kdj.k vkSj fj;k;rxzkgh ds chp 18-04-2002 dks fd, x, djkj esa ifjHkkf"kr fd;k x;k gS] mudk vFkZ ogha gksxk tks mUgsa Øe’k% mDr djkj esa fn;k x;k gSA [Qk- la- Hkkjkjkizk@rd-@fnYyh&xqM+xkao@jkjk&8@2013&14] vyds’k 'kekZ] la;qDr lfpo MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 19th February, 2014 S.O. 476(E).—Whereas, by the notification of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Surface Transport, number S.O.660(E), dated the 27th September, 1996 and S.O.239(E) dated 24th March,1998, issued under section 11 of the National Highways Authority of India Act,1988 (68 of 1988), the Central Government had entrusted the stretches from km. 36.630 to km 162.500 in the States of Haryana and Rajasthan and from km. 11.000 to km. 36.630 in the National Capital Territory of Delhi and the State of Haryana, of the National Highway No. 8 to the National Highways Authority of India (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”). And whereas, the Authority had entered into a concession agreement on 18th April, 2002 (hereinafter referred to as “agreement”) with M/s. JAYPEE DSC VENTURES LIMITED (now Delhi Gurgaon Super Connectivity Limited i.e. DGSCL) having its registered office at Dundahera, Gurgaon-Delhi Border, Km. 24 Milestone, NH-8, Gurgaon – 122016 Also at E-9, 3rd Floor, South Extension-II, New Delhi–110049 (hereinafter referred to as “the Concessionaire”) for the development and maintenance of National Highway No. 8 from km 14.300 to km 24.000 in the National Capital Territory of Delhi and from km. 24.000 to km. 42.000 in the State of Haryana (hereinafter referred to as the “said section”). And whereas, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways (Collection of Fees by any person for the use of section of National Highway/Permanent Bridge/Temporary Bridge on National Highway) Rules, 1997, the Central Government by notification of the Government of India vide S.O. No. 994 (E) dated 18th June, 2007 authorized the Concessionaire for levying and collecting fees on the mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section at the rates and at the toll plazas specified in its schedule. And whereas, Hon’ble High Court of Delhi vide Order dated 19th February, 2014 in OMP No. 288/2013 (filed by DGSCL) and Execution Petition No. 88/2013 (filed by IDFC on behalf of the Senior Lenders) observed that the Authority, the Concessionaire and the Senior Lenders have agreed that DGSCL be substituted in the said Concession Agreement by SPV formed by the Senior Lenders (IDFC & Others) as a Selectee. And the liabilities and obligations of DGSCL are taken over and assumed by IDFC and upon appointment of a new Selectee, the said obligations and liabilities shall be assumed and taken over by the said new Selectee. It is further agreed between the parties that as the revised toll notification becomes effective, no toll or user fee shall be collected from km. 24.000 toll plaza and the said toll plaza shall be -- 4 of 7 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(ii)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 5 dismantled and accordingly, the user fee at km. 42.000 toll plaza now shall be the sum of user fee at km. 24.000 toll plaza and that at km. 42.000 toll plaza previously. Now, therefore, in view of the directions issued by the Hon’ble High Court of Delhi vide Order dated 19th February, 2014 in OMP No. 288/2013 and Execution Petition No. 88/2013, the Central Government, hereby notifies that there shall be levied and collected fee on the mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section at the rates and toll plazas specified in the schedule below and authorizes the Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire to collect and retain the fees from the date of publication of this notification in the Official Gazette and until the termination date as specified in the agreement. SCHEDULE The Fee shall be collected at two toll plazas, one at the end of the project at km 42.000 (sum of the user fee at km. 24.000 toll plaza and that at km. 42.000 toll plaza previously) of NH-8 (Kherki Daula village, District Gurgaon in the state of Haryana) and second as side plaza at km. 19.100 of NH-8 (at the intersection with Indira Gandhi International Airport road in the state of NCT of Delhi). The aforesaid fee at columns (3), (4) and (5) shall be revised at the beginning of each financial year with effect from 1st April every year. The increase will be based on a fee revision Index Computed to two decimal places which has a value of 1.00 as on April 1, 2005 and is increased based on wholesale price index as on 1st April every year. The revised/actual fee shall be computed as follows : Revised/Actual Fee = Base Fee × WPI-B WPI-A Where WPI-A = is the Wholesale Price Index as on 1st April, 2005 i.e. 191 WPI-B = is the Wholesale Price Index as on 31st December preceding the fee revision date. The actual Fees to be charged shall be rounded off to the nearest one rupee. 2. The following vehicles shall be exempted from payment of user fee :— “Ambulances, fire tenders and official vehicles transporting and accompanying the President of India, the Vice President of India, the Prime Minister of India, Ministers of Government of India, Governors, Lt Governor, Chief Ministers, Presiding Officers of Central and State Legislatures having jurisdictions, Ministers of State Government, Judges of the Supreme Court of India and of the High Court having jurisdiction, Secretaries and -- 5 of 7 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] Commissioner of State Government, Foreign Dignitaries on State visit to India, Heads of Foreign Missions stationed in India using cars with CD symbol, Executive Magistrates, officers of Ministry of Road Transport & Highways and National Highways Authority of India, person required to use the project for discharging their statutory obligations in relation to the site, Independent Consultant and the Central and State Forces in uniform including armed forces, para military forces and police”. 3. The rates of Fees, the Categories of vehicles exempted from payment of fee and the name, address and telephone number of the Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire to whom complaints, if any, should be addressed, shall be conspicuously and prominently displayed 500 metres ahead of the toll booth, 100 metres ahead of the toll booth and at the toll booth also. The height of the display boards and size of letters being such that it is easy for drivers to read the display boards. 4. A table of fees authorized to be collected on any section of National Highway/Permanent Bridge/Temporary Bridge on National Highway shall be put up by such person in a conspicuous place near a fee collection booth legibly written or printed in English, Hindi and the regional language of the area in which the National Highway is situated. 5. The Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire shall nominate an official as in-charge of fee collection and official so nominated shall be responsible to ensure that fees is collected at not more than agreed rates and the Fee collection is smooth without causing undue hardship to the road users, and for all other matters connected therewith. 6. The Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire shall not collect any fees from local personal traffic in excess of 50% of the applicable fees for the specific category of vehicle and from local commercial traffic in excess of 66% of the applicable fee for the specific category of vehicle at the two toll plazas as under provided the local traffic take a pass for 40 trips valid for 30 days. For the purpose of this notification, unless the context otherwise requires :— (i) “Local commercial traffic” means any commercial vehicle including buses, trucks, light motor vehicles or taxis engaged in carrying goods and passengers registered with the Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire at one of the toll plazas as plying routinely on the project highway without crossing more than one of the toll plazas. (ii) “Local personal traffic” means and includes a personal vehicle which is registered with the Concessionaire at one of the toll plazas as plying routinely on the project highway without crossing more than one of the toll plazas. (iii) “Termination Date” means the date on which the said agreement entered into between the Authority and the Receiver/Selectee/New Concessionaire expires pursuant to the provisions of the said agreement or is terminated by a termination notice. (iv) The words and expression used in this notification and not defined but defined in the agreement dated 18.04.2002 entered into between the Authority and the Concessionaire in respect of section from Km. 14.300 to Km. 42.000 of the National Highway No. 8 shall have the same meanings as assigned to them respectively in the said agreement.
